word: G1863 - ἐπάγω - epágō - ep-ag'-o - Bring upon

sections.paragraph:
- Original: ἐπάγω

- Transliteration: Epago

- Phonetic: ep-ag'-o

- Definition:

1. to lead or bring upon

2. to bring a thing on one

a. to cause something to befall one, usually something evil

- Origin: from G1909 and G71

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G1909 and G71; to superinduce that is inflict (an evil) charge (a crime): - bring upon.
